data.content:
The file is generated successfully but not included in compilation. This is the classic MSBuild "generated file not in Compile items" problem. The default `**/*.cs` glob expands at evaluation time (before the target runs), so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to the `Compile` item group (and `FileWrites` for clean support) inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and mark for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's default glob (`**/*.cs`) is evaluated at evaluation time, before any targets run, so the generated file isn't picked up.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, and also to `FileWrites` so it gets cleaned. Let me invoke the skill for this.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and mark for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly. Here's the diagnosis:

**Root cause:** MSBuild's default `**/*.cs` glob expands at **evaluation time** — before any targets execute. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file during **execution time**, so the compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="..."/>` and `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` inside an `<ItemGroup>` within the target. This explicitly tells the compiler about the generated file and ensures `dotnet clean` removes it.
